patch.value: Launch content: flagship doctrine essay, 90-second demo video, technical articles, comparison/complement pages, single-message channel plan

One central narrative everywhere: 'Project management is context management' (coding gets cheaper; project state, coordination, intent, and evidence stay scarce; a backlog is not automatically useful context; agents need context selected for the current decision that survives sessions/models/tools/people). Blocked on the Phase-1 rewrite — never send launch traffic to contradictory positioning.

Flagship essay 'Project Management Is Context Management' written as an opinionated technical manifesto, not a disguised product announcement; demo video 'Your agent lost its context. Here is how it resumes in 60 seconds' (fresh session -> pm context -> pm next + reason -> claim -> linked evidence -> handoff); three technical articles (context tested like search infrastructure: signals/nDCG/recall/budgets/CI gates; a task list is not a coordination protocol: ownership/claim races/deps/atomic history; build a context-aware tool with the pm SDK: define -> preflight -> test); comparison pages (vs Beads, vs Backlog.md, vs Task Master) and complement pages (with OpenSpec, with Spec Kit, with GitHub Issues, with Jira/Linear) — complement pages prioritized as more credible and shareable; channel rollout ordered by immediate-install ability (GitHub/npm metadata, MCP Registry, skills catalogs, plugin marketplaces, MCP-client integration docs, Show HN with reproducible demo, developer communities, terminal-demo clips, context-engineering talks) with every surface leading the same category and doctrine
